content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Mike B., Ted, Jay, Bob S., Nan,
    John K., Natalie, Ken F., John G.

Date : May 18, 1977

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
      Gina

Subject: Inquiry from ORANGE COUNTY VOLUNTEER IN POLITICS
         PROGRAM (VIP)


Dennis Desnel (714/835-7460) is the coordinator of the above
mentioned program which is an agent for the Central Committee
of Orange County and reports accordingly using the I.D.
number issued to the Central Committee.

Individuals contribute money to the VIP earmarking how funds
should be contributed either to specific candidates, a caucus,
etc. VIP in turn makes the contributions.

The amount of the contributions total $60 per year per contributor. $20 (1/3) is kept by VIP for overhead and $40 (2/3) is
passed on to the designated recipient. The recipient then lists
VIP as intermediary along with the name of the contributor.
Their question is, may they consider the contribution $40
instead of $60 and not itemize on their reports all the contributors which requires detailed records and threatens to increase
the cost of the overhead.

CTA is involved in a similar situation. It collects funds for the
PAC retaining a percentage for overhead. We have advised CTA
that the amount deducted and retained by them for overhead is not
a contribution reportable by either CTA or the PAC.

The difference, of course, is that CTA is not a primary purpose
committee and VIP appears to be. Is there any way that VIP could
establish itself as a multipurpose committee and, therefore, not
be required to report overhead perhaps by simply separating itself
from the Central Committee? Do we want to arrive at that
conclusion?

Please let me know your opinion.

The contributor has made a $20 contribution to
VIP and a $40 contribution to the designated candidate.
VIP is an intermediary with a respect to the $40 contribution.

148

sections.question_synthetic: The memo requests FPPC staff opinion on whether the Orange County Volunteer in Politics Program (VIP), which collects and distributes earmarked contributions on behalf of the Central Committee while retaining a portion for overhead, can be structured as a multi-purpose committee to avoid itemization reporting requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal memorandum requesting staff opinion rather than an FPPC advisory letter response; document is heavily redacted/faded making some text illegible; references prior CTA (likely California Teachers Association) advice; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; appears to be seeking guidance on structuring options rather than providing definitive advice

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The memo requests FPPC staff opinion on whether the Orange County Volunteer in Politics Program (VIP), which collects and distributes earmarked contributions on behalf of the Central Committee while retaining a portion for overhead, can be structured as a multi-purpose committee to avoid itemization reporting requirements.

embedding.summary: This is an internal FPPC memorandum from staff requesting guidance on whether VIP, an agent of the Orange County Central Committee that collects earmarked contributions and retains a portion for overhead expenses, could be restructured as a multi-purpose committee to reduce reporting burdens.
